author: @마하-x9x
text:
I think that engaging in sexual activity or sex trafficking with someone while knowing they are a minor is definitely a crime. Your videos exposing people who commit such crimes are excellent. But in this video and in other similar videos, there was no sex or sex trafficking, right? You were just communicating with a child. By making videos like this, using child-related content as a pretext to report to the police or label someone as a "horrible person," it seems like you are just inflating a situation that isn’t really a problem to get views and then using the police or public opinion as a shield to cover it up. This is advice for you. You may have many subscribers and views now, but if you keep entrapping people who haven’t committed crimes for profit, you might regret it greatly someday.
Many Americans are not aware, but in the case of videos featuring children, while they are classified as serious crimes in the United States, in other countries they are often not considered serious crimes unless the person actually produced or sold them. Honestly, I think that’s reasonable. In countries outside the U.S., many people would think it is unusual to classify someone as committing a serious crime just for collecting or watching videos found online that they did not personally create.
